en: Avetta PQF - US Supplier Diversity Certification

If you answer yes to this question you will be asked to provide your certificate for one or more of the following:
    Supplier Diversity Certifications can be issued by the Small Business Administration (SBA) and/or national, state, or local associations. Here are some resources: Small Business Administration (SBA), Federal Reserve Minority-Owned Business - Minorities must own and control at least 51% of the business Minority owner(s) must serve as President or CEO (if both positions exist) Minority owner(s) must be active in daily management Minority owner(s) must be a U.S. citizen Minority owner(s) must have the ownership and officer position for at least 6 months Women-Owned Business - The business is at least 51% owned, controlled, operated, and managed by a woman or women Veteran-Owned Business - The business is at least 51% owned, operated, managed, and controlled by veterans Veteran Disability-Owned Business Enterprise - A business which is 51% owned, controlled, operated, and managed by a veteran, but disability was not incurred during their time of service. Service-Disabled Veteran Disability-Owned Business Enterprise - A business which is 51% owned, controlled, operated, and managed by a veteran, who sustained their disability during their time of service. LGBTQ+ Owned Business - The business is at least 51% owned, operated, managed, and controlled by a person or persons who identify as part of the LGBTQ+ community and are either a U.S. Citizen or a lawful permanent resident Native American-Owned Business - The business must be owned, operated, and controlled by at least 51% Native American/Alaskan/Hawaiian Ownership. Disability-Owned Business Enterprise - A business which is 51% owned, controlled, operated, and managed by a person(s) with a disability.
Does your company hold any current US Diversity Certifications listed below?

Minority-Owned Business (African American, Asian America, Hispanic Americans, Native Americans, Pacific Islanders)

8(a) Business Development Program - To qualify for the 8(a) program, businesses must meet the following eligibility criteria: Be a small business Not have previously participated in the 8(a) program Be at least 51% owned and controlled by U.S. citizens who are socially and economically disadvantaged Have a personal net worth of $850 thousand or less, adjusted gross income of $400 thousand or less, and assets totaling $6.5 million or less Demonstrate good character Demonstrate the potential for success such as having been in business for two years

This certification may be obtained through the Small Business Administration or other certifying body. To obtain this certificate, a Small Business has to meet certain size standards based on average annual receipts or the average number of employees based on the type of trades you perform. You can also use the Table of Size Standards. You can use the SBA’s Size Standards Tool to help you determine if you are a Small Business.\n\nIf you select yes to this question, you will be required to upload your certification from the SBA or other certifying body or upload a copy of your self-certification.
